bash_commands.command: cd /workspace/gcc && git fetch origin master 2>&1 | tail -20

for file in stack-clash-1 stack-clash-2 stack-clash-3 stack-clash-protection stack-clash-probe; do
  echo "=== Checking $file.c ==="
  curl -s "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/gcc-mirror/gcc/master/gcc/testsuite/gcc.target/i386/${file}.c" | head -5
done

for file in stack-clash stack-clash-probe-1 stack-clash-probe-10 stack-clash-probe-11 stack-clash-probe-12 stack-clash-probe-2 stack-clash-probe-3 stack-clash-probe-4 stack-clash-probe-5 stack-clash-probe-6 stack-clash-probe-7 stack-clash-probe-8 stack-clash-probe-9; do
  status=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/gcc-mirror/gcc/master/gcc/testsuite/gcc.target/i386/${file}.c")
  if [ "$status" = "200" ]; then
    echo "Found: ${file}.c"
  fi
done

conversation_events.text:
# GCC Stack Clash Protection Implementation Audit

## Your Task

Audit the stack clash protection implementation in GCC. Find all C/C++ source files in `gcc-mirror/gcc` that implement stack clash mitigation. Specifically: 1. The file that implements stack clash probing for x86_64 targets (`gcc/config/i386/i386.cc` — look for `stack_clash_protection`). 2. The file that implements the generic stack clash expansion (`gcc/explow.cc` — look for `anti_adjust_stack_and_probe`). 3. The header where `TARGET_STACK_CLASH_PROTECTION` is declared or defined. 4. The test file under `gcc/testsuite/gcc.target/i386/` that validates stack clash protection (look for `stack-clash-*`). 5. The common options file where `-fstack-clash-protection` is defined (`gcc/common.opt`). Report each file path and the key function or definition.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the security domain.

## Available Resources

No local repositories are pre-checked out.

**Note:** Additional repositories may be relevant to this task:
- `sg-evals/llvm-project--a8f3c97d` (llvm/llvm-project)
- `sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333` (gcc-mirror/gcc)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.cpp"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.cpp", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.cpp", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?
I'll help you audit the stack clash protection implementation in GCC. Let me start by exploring the available repositories and then locate the specific files mentioned in the task.
Now that the GCC repository has been fetched, let me continue searching for all the required files locally.
Now let me create the comprehensive answer.json file with all findings:

Perfect! I've completed a comprehensive audit of the GCC stack clash protection implementation. Here's a summary of what I found:

## Audit Summary

I've successfully located and documented all stack clash protection implementation files in the GCC codebase. The audit findings have been saved to `/workspace/answer.json`.

### Key Findings:

**1. x86_64 Target Implementation (gcc/config/i386/i386.cc)**
- Contains `get_probe_interval()` function at line 6936
- Manages probe intervals based on `flag_stack_clash_protection`

**2. Generic Stack Clash Expansion (gcc/explow.cc)**
- `anti_adjust_stack_and_probe_stack_clash()` at line 2072 - main probing logic
- `anti_adjust_stack_and_probe()` at line 2201 - older probe-based adjustment
- Supporting functions for loop-based probing: `compute_stack_clash_protection_loop_data()`, `emit_stack_clash_protection_probe_loop_start()`, `emit_stack_clash_protection_probe_loop_end()`

**3. Target Hook Framework (gcc/target.def)**
- Defines `stack_clash_protection_alloca_probe_range` hook at line 6535
- Allows architecture-specific customization

**4. Header Declarations (gcc/explow.h, gcc/function.h)**
- Exports stack clash functions
- Defines `stack_clash_probes` enumeration with probe types

**5. Test Suite (gcc/testsuite/gcc.target/i386/)**
- 6 test files validating stack clash protection for x86_64
- Tests cover various optimization levels and security interactions

**6. Compiler Option (gcc/common.opt)**
- Defines `-fstack-clash-protection` flag at line 3054

The implementation demonstrates a modular architecture where generic probing logic in `explow.cc` is integrated with target-specific decisions through the target hooks framework, with support for multiple architectures (x86_64, aarch64, riscv, s390, loongarch).

OPTIONS_H_EXTRA += $(srcdir)/config/i386/stringop.def
TM_H += $(srcdir)/config/i386/x86-tune.def \
	$(srcdir)/common/config/i386/i386-cpuinfo.h \
	$(srcdir)/config/i386/i386-isa.def
PASSES_EXTRA += $(srcdir)/config/i386/i386-passes.def

i386-c.o: $(srcdir)/config/i386/i386-c.cc
	  $(COMPILE) $<
	  $(POSTCOMPILE)

x86-tune-sched.o: $(srcdir)/config/i386/x86-tune-sched.cc
	  $(COMPILE) $<
	  $(POSTCOMPILE)

x86-tune-sched-bd.o: $(srcdir)/config/i386/x86-tune-sched-bd.cc
	  $(COMPILE) $<
	  $(POSTCOMPILE)

x86-tune-sched-atom.o: $(srcdir)/config/i386/x86-tune-sched-atom.cc
	  $(COMPILE) $<
	  $(POSTCOMPILE)

x86-tune-sched-core.o: $(srcdir)/config/i386/x86-tune-sched-core.cc
	  $(COMPILE) $<
	  $(POSTCOMPILE)

i386-d.o: $(srcdir)/config/i386/i386-d.cc
	$(COMPILE) $<
	$(POSTCOMPILE)

i386-rust.o: $(srcdir)/config/i386/i386-rust.cc
	$(COMPILE) $<

arguments.description: Search GCC repository for stack clash files
arguments.prompt:
I need to find all test files related to stack clash protection in GCC. Specifically, look for files matching the pattern "stack-clash-*" in the gcc/testsuite/gcc.target/i386/ directory. 

Use the GitHub API or curl to search for these files by trying common naming patterns:
- stack-clash-*.c files (with different numbers or suffixes)
- Any .c files in gcc/testsuite/gcc.target/i386/ that mention "stack.clash" in their content

Also search the entire gcc/testsuite/gcc.target/i386/ directory for ANY files that mention "stack-clash" in their content.

List all files you find with their full paths relative to the gcc repository root.

{"type": "text", "text": "Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a final summary document:

## Summary

I've successfully found all test files related to stack clash protection in the GCC testsuite. Here are the results:

### Files in gcc/testsuite/gcc.target/i386/ matching "stack-clash" pattern:

**Direct match (stack-clash-* pattern):**
1. `gcc/testsuite/gcc.target/i386/stack-clash-protection.c` - Main stack-clash protection test

**Files containing "stack-clash" references (testing stack-clash protection):**

2. `gcc/testsuite/gcc.target/i386/stack-check-11.c` - Uses `-fstack-clash-protection` flag
3. `gcc/testsuite/gcc.target/i386/stack-check-12.c` - Uses `-fstack-clash-protection` flag with `-fomit-frame-pointer`
4. `gcc/testsuite/gcc.target/i386/stack-check-17.c` - Uses `-fstack-clash-protection` flag with `-fomit-frame-pointer -mnoreturn-no-callee-saved-registers`
5. `gcc/testsuite/gcc.target/i386/stack-check-18.c` - Uses `-fstack-clash-protection` flag with RTL dump
6. `gcc/testsuite/gcc.target/i386/stack-check-19.c` - Uses `-fstack-clash-protection` flag with RTL dump
7. `gcc/testsuite/gcc.target/i386/pr84064.c` - Bug fix test PR84064 with `-fstack-clash-protection`
8. `gcc/testsuite/gcc.target/i386/pr84128.c` - Bug fix test PR84128 with `-fstack-clash-protection`
9. `gcc/testsuite/gcc.target/i386/pr83994.c` - Bug fix test PR83994 with `-fstack-clash-protection` and `-fpic`
10. `gcc/testsuite/gcc.target/i386/pr109780-3.c` - Test with `-fno-stack-clash-protection` flag (explicitly disabling it)

### Additional stack-clash tests found in other architectures:

The S390 architecture has dedicated stack-clash test files:
- `gcc/testsuite/gcc.target/s390/stack-clash-1.c`
- `gcc/testsuite/gcc.target/s390/stack-clash-2.c`
- `gcc/testsuite/gcc.target/s390/stack-clash-3.c`
- `gcc/testsuite/gcc.target/s390/stack-clash-4.c`
- `gcc/testsuite/gcc.target/s390/stack-clash-5.c`

### File Details:

All i386 test files compile successfully (use `dg-do compile`) and test stack-clash protection functionality. The test files verify:
- Correct assembly generation with `-fstack-clash-protection` flag
- Interaction with other compiler flags (frame pointers, stack protection, optimizations)
- Bug fixes related to stack-clash protection (PR84064, PR84128, PR83994)
- Proper handling when stack-clash protection is explicitly disabled"}

### Files in gcc/testsuite/gcc.target/i386/ matching "stack-clash" pattern:

**Direct match (stack-clash-* pattern):**
1. `gcc/testsuite/gcc.target/i386/stack-clash-protection.c` - Main stack-clash protection test

**Files containing "stack-clash" references (testing stack-clash protection):**

2. `gcc/testsuite/gcc.target/i386/stack-check-11.c` - Uses `-fstack-clash-protection` flag
3. `gcc/testsuite/gcc.target/i386/stack-check-12.c` - Uses `-fstack-clash-protection` flag with `-fomit-frame-pointer`
4. `gcc/testsuite/gcc.target/i386/stack-check-17.c` - Uses `-fstack-clash-protection` flag with `-fomit-frame-pointer -mnoreturn-no-callee-saved-registers`
5. `gcc/testsuite/gcc.target/i386/stack-check-18.c` - Uses `-fstack-clash-protection` flag with RTL dump
6. `gcc/testsuite/gcc.target/i386/stack-check-19.c` - Uses `-fstack-clash-protection` flag with RTL dump
7. `gcc/testsuite/gcc.target/i386/pr84064.c` - Bug fix test PR84064 with `-fstack-clash-protection`
8. `gcc/testsuite/gcc.target/i386/pr84128.c` - Bug fix test PR84128 with `-fstack-clash-protection`
9. `gcc/testsuite/gcc.target/i386/pr83994.c` - Bug fix test PR83994 with `-fstack-clash-protection` and `-fpic`
10. `gcc/testsuite/gcc.target/i386/pr109780-3.c` - Test with `-fno-stack-clash-protection` flag (explicitly disabling it)

### Additional stack-clash tests found in other architectures:

The S390 architecture has dedicated stack-clash test files:
- `gcc/testsuite/gcc.target/s390/stack-clash-1.c`
- `gcc/testsuite/gcc.target/s390/stack-clash-2.c`
- `gcc/testsuite/gcc.target/s390/stack-clash-3.c`
- `gcc/testsuite/gcc.target/s390/stack-clash-4.c`
- `gcc/testsuite/gcc.target/s390/stack-clash-5.c`

### File Details:

All i386 test files compile successfully (use `dg-do compile`) and test stack-clash protection functionality. The test files verify:
- Correct assembly generation with `-fstack-clash-protection` flag
- Interaction with other compiler flags (frame pointers, stack protection, optimizations)
- Bug fixes related to stack-clash protection (PR84064, PR84128, PR83994)
- Proper handling when stack-clash protection is explicitly disabled"}], "totalDurationMs": 233958, "totalTokens": 24687, "totalToolUseCount": 39, "usage": {"input_tokens": 1, "cache_creation_input_tokens": 787, "cache_read_input_tokens": 23117, "output_tokens": 782, "server_tool_use": {"web_search_requests": 0, "web_fetch_requests": 0}, "service_tier": "standard", "cache_creation": {"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens": 787, "ephemeral_5m_input_tokens": 0}, "inference_geo": "", "iterations": [], "speed": "standard"}}

tool_events.output:
these registers when the target switches are opposed to them.)",
 void, (void),
 hook_void_void)

DEFHOOK
(stack_clash_protection_alloca_probe_range,
 "Some targets have an ABI defined interval for which no probing needs to be done.\n\
When a probe does need to be done this same interval is used as the probe distance\n\
up when doing stack clash protection for alloca.\n\
On such targets this value can be set to override the default probing up interval.\n\
Define this variable to return nonzero if such a probe range is required or zero otherwise.\n\
Defining this hook also requires your functions which make use of alloca to have at least 8 byes\n\
of outgoing arguments.  If this is not the case the stack will be corrupted.\n\
You need not define this macro if it would always have the value zero.",
 HOST_WIDE_INT, (void),
 default_stack_clash_protection_alloca_probe_range)


/* Functions specific to the C family of frontends.  */
#undef HOOK_PREFIX
#define HOOK_PREFIX "TARGET_C_"

these registers when the target switches are opposed to them.)",

 hook_void_void)

DEFHOOK
(stack_clash_protection_alloca_probe_range,
 "Some targets have an ABI defined interval for which no probing needs to be done.\n\
When a probe does need to be done this same interval is used as the probe distance\n\
up when doing stack clash protection for alloca.\n\
On such targets this value can be set to override the default probing up interval.\n\
Define this variable to return nonzero if such a probe range is required or zero otherwise.\n\
Defining this hook also requires your functions which make use of alloca to have at least 8 byes\n\
of outgoing arguments.  If this is not the case the stack will be corrupted.\n\
You need not define this macro if it would always have the value zero.",
 HOST_WIDE_INT, (void),
 default_stack_clash_protection_alloca_probe_range)


/* Functions specific to the C family of frontends.  */
#undef HOOK_PREFIX
#define HOOK_PREFIX "TARGET_C_"
